copy.title: Spurs outlast Thunder in Game 7. Cole leading Schwab. Microsoft soars five percent.
copy.keyTakeaway: Spurs advance past Thunder in a seven-game West Finals slugfest. Tech stocks mixed as Microsoft surges while chip names falter. Culture losing a legend: Marcia Lucas, the Oscar-winning editor behind Star Wars and American Graffiti, has died at 80.

todaysHits.sports: Spurs close out Thunder 111-103 in Game 7 Western Finals.
todaysHits.markets: SPY flat, QQQ up 0.4%, MSFT soars 5.4%.
todaysHits.golf: Eric Cole leads Charles Schwab Challenge at 12-under.
todaysHits.culture: Marcia Lucas, Oscar-winning Star Wars editor, dies at 80.

lead.headline: Spurs outlast Thunder in absolute war for West Finals
lead.whatHappened: San Antonio just survived a seven-game bloodbath against Oklahoma City, winning 111-103 in Game 7 to advance out of the West. Both teams left everything on the floor across that series, and the Spurs found a way to close it out when it mattered most.
lead.whyBullet1: This was a heavyweight matchup between two legitimate contenders, and San Antonio proved they can win the grind games when the margin for error disappears.
lead.whyBullet2: The Thunder had their chance to punch through but couldn't finish in Game 7, which means their window stays open but gets tighter.
lead.whatToSay: That Spurs team just went through hell and came out the other side. They're dangerous right now.

markets.mood: Microsoft's 5.4% jump carried the whole market higher, but nvidia and tesla both tanked—big tech is splitting into winners and losers instead of moving as one block.
markets.whyBullet1: If your 401k is heavy on index funds like SPY, you're up today, but the breadth is getting thinner, which means fewer stocks are actually doing the work.
markets.whyBullet2: Watch for earnings whispers Thursday and Friday—if software and cloud companies (the MSFT play) keep outrunning hardware, that split gets worse and portfolio diversification actually matters again.
markets.bringUp: Microsoft's up 5.4% while nvidia's down 1.5 on the same day—that's the first real crack we've seen in the 'all big tech rises together' trade that's dominated since January.

golf.headline: Eric Cole chasing first PGA win at Schwab Challenge.
golf.whyCare1: Charles Schwab Challenge is a full FedEx Cup event — wins here count heavy toward the playoffs.
golf.whyCare2: Cole's at 12-under with Ryan Gerard just one shot back, so this is legitimately tight heading into the weekend.
golf.watchFor: Whether Cole can hold at 12-under or if Gerard makes a run — Gerard's only one back and the field behind them is stacked.
golf.whatToSay: Eric Cole's been knocking on the door all year. This could be the week he finally breaks through at a big event.

culture.topic: Bill Maher torched Trump's concert disaster
culture.whatHappened: Bill Maher went after Trump's Freedom 250 concert on his show, mocking it as a complete disaster.
culture.whyItMatters: Maher's got a platform and he's using it to call out what he sees as a failed political event — that kind of public ribbing sticks around.
culture.whatToSay: Did you see what Maher said about that concert? Total bloodbath.

culture.topic: Golden Knights face Hurricanes for Stanley Cup
culture.whatHappened: Vegas made the Stanley Cup Final and will play Carolina's Hurricanes for all the hardware.
culture.whyItMatters: This is the Finals. Two teams, one cup, six weeks of hockey that actually matters.
culture.whatToSay: Golden Knights are in the Finals again. You watching?

culture.whatHappened: The show about surgical work-life separation — employees literally split their memories at the office door — came back and picked up exactly where it left off.

copy.finalSharpTake: The Spurs just proved you don't need stars to win in the West anymore—San Antonio's depth got past OKC's firepower in a Game 7 grind. That shift ripples through the league: if a constructed roster beats a talented one, front offices stop chasing names and start building systems. On markets, Microsoft's five-percent pop signals serious money moving into AI infrastructure, while Tesla and Nvidia got punished today. That's your actual tell: money's picking winners in the AI space, not betting the whole sector goes up.

glance.sports: Spurs survived Game 7 against OKC, 111–103, to advance out of the West after a brutal seven-game series.
glance.market: Tech mixed: MSFT crushed it with +5.4%, but NVDA and TSLA both tanked over 1%, while SPY stayed flat at +0.2%.
glance.bestConvo: San Antonio just won a Game 7 on the road. That's not supposed to happen in 2026. Ask someone if they watched it.
glance.watchNext: Eric Cole is leading the Charles Schwab Challenge at -12 with rounds left to play—watch if he closes it out or if someone else sneaks up.
glance.quickRec: If you own tech, today was a reminder that big moves happen within the same sector on the same day. Don't assume the whole market moved.
